config_changes: enable_dns_hostnames -> dns_servers (custom DNS, Azure provides DNS by default)
migration_mapping.notes: Direct 1:1 equivalent. Address space uses CIDR notation. Azure VNet requires至少一个address_space prefix.

config_changes: No direct EIP association needed in Azure
Automatically attached to VNet in Azure
migration_mapping.notes: Direct equivalent. Azure Internet Gateway is a native VNet resource, not a separate attachment.

config_changes: No separate route table association (handled by route table resource)
AWS subnet ID reference -> Azure subnet ID reference
migration_mapping.notes: In Azure, public/private designation is determined by whether subnet has NAT gateway or is associated with route tables directing to virtual network gateway.

config_changes: Private subnets route to NAT Gateway in private route table
No public IP assignment on launch
route blocks map to route resource blocks in Azure
Gateway VNet attachment for virtual network gateway routes
Direct equivalents for local and internet routes
migration_mapping.notes: Direct 1:1 mapping. Route tables in Azure associate with subnets similarly to AWS.
Direct 1:1 mapping. Azure uses separate association resource.

config_changes: Azure does not support VPC-level domain concept
migration_mapping.notes: Functional equivalent for NAT Gateway EIP. In Azure, public IPs are standalone resources and can be reassigned. Standard SKU provides static allocation.

config_changes: Azure NAT Gateway has no deletion protection (AWS has it by default)
Azure NAT Gateway is a separate resource, not embedded in subnet
migration_mapping.notes: Direct 1:1 equivalent. Azure NAT Gateway is a regional service, not attached to individual subnets. Supports multiple subnets via subnet associations.
Direct equivalent for Linux VMs. Requires separate network interface with IP configuration.
Private instances in Azure have no public IP. Can access internet via NAT Gateway.

config_changes: vpc_id -> network_security_group doesn't require VNet reference
migration_mapping.notes: Direct functional equivalent. In Azure, NSG attaches to subnet or NIC, not auto-applied to resources in subnet.

config_changes: assume_role_policy -> Azure MSI with role assignments
Service principal equivalent via user assigned identity
Role assignment to resources via azurerm_role_assignment
migration_mapping.notes: Functional equivalent. Azure uses Managed Identity (MSI) for VM authentication. User Assigned Identity provides persistent identity across VM recreates.

config_changes: No equivalent - Azure uses Managed Identity natively
migration_mapping.notes: Retain as-is not applicable - this resource doesn't exist in Azure. Azure VM uses user_assigned_identity_id or system_assigned_identity for IAM.

config_changes: internal = false -> frontend_ip_configuration with public IP for public LB
migration_mapping.notes: Direct equivalent for Network Load Balancer. Azure NLB uses frontend IP + backend pools. SKU determines feature set.

config_changes: port -> port in azurerm_lb_rule (not in pool)
vpc_id -> network_interface_ids or azurerm_lb_backend_address_poolassociated with VMs
migration_mapping.notes: Direct equivalent. Azure backend address pool references network interfaces from VMs.
Functional equivalent. Azure associates backend pools with network interfaces on VMs, not directly with VM IDs.

config_changes: No direct listener equivalent - rule handles routing
migration_mapping.notes: Direct functional equivalent. Azure LB rule combines listener and target group attachment. Requires azurerm_lb_probe for health checks.

architecture_decisions.title: VNet Address Space Design
architecture_decisions.context: AWS VPC uses single CIDR block. Azure VNet requires address_space with multiple prefixes supported.
architecture_decisions.decision: Use the AWS VPC CIDR as the primary address_space prefix in Azure VNet. Reserve address space for future expansion and PaaS endpoints.
alternatives_considered: Single address prefix matching VPC CIDR
Multiple address prefixes for RFC1918 + future expansion
consequences: Positive: Direct IP range preservation simplifies migration
Negative: May need to adjust on-prem connectivity
Need to verify no IP overlap with existing Azure resources

architecture_decisions.title: NAT Gateway Redundancy Design
architecture_decisions.context: AWS NAT Gateway is a single-AZ resource. Azure NAT Gateway is regional and scales automatically.
architecture_decisions.decision: Use single Azure NAT Gateway resource with subnet associations to all private subnets. No need for multi-AZ NAT Gateway deployment.
alternatives_considered: Single NAT Gateway serving all private subnets
Multiple NAT Gateways per availability zone
Use Internet egress via virtual network appliance
consequences: Positive: Simplified architecture, eliminates need for multi-AZ NAT
Negative: No multi-AZ redundancy for NAT Gateway (Azure handled)
Need to ensure public IP redundancy via SKU selection

architecture_decisions.title: Managed Identity Strategy
architecture_decisions.context: AWS uses IAM instance profiles attached to EC2. Azure uses Managed Identities.
architecture_decisions.decision: Deploy User Assigned Identity for persistent permissions across VM deployments. Associate with Virtual Machines for application access.
alternatives_considered: System Assigned Identity (simpler, tied to VM lifecycle)
User Assigned Identity (persistent, shareable across VMs)
Service Principal with service principal credentials
consequences: Positive: No credential management needed (rotation handled by Azure)
Negative: User Assigned Identity requires separate resource
Need to update application code to use IMDS endpoint

architecture_decisions.title: Load Balancer Health Check Strategy
architecture_decisions.context: AWS target groups have implicit health checks. Azure LB rules require explicit probe configuration.
architecture_decisions.decision: Create azurerm_lb_probe for each LB rule with HTTP/TCP health check configuration. Replicate AWS target group health check settings.
alternatives_considered: TCP probe on same port as traffic
HTTP GET probe for application health
consequences: Need to migrate health check path from AWS target group

architecture_decisions.title: Network Security Group Rule Migration
architecture_decisions.context: AWS security groups attach to ENI. Azure NSGs attach to subnet or NIC.
architecture_decisions.decision: Associate NSG with subnets for network-layer security. Also associate with NICs for explicit VM-level rules.
alternatives_considered: NSG on both subnet and NIC
consequences: Positive: Defense in depth with both subnet and NIC-level security
Negative: Potential rule duplication if not careful
Need to review all AWS SG rules and assign appropriately

redesign_required.redesign_rationale: Azure doesn't have instance profile concept. Managed Identity replaces this entirely.
redesign_required.alternative: Use azurerm_user_assigned_identity for persistent identity, assign roles via azurerm_role_assignment
redesign_required.impact: Low - application code needs to authenticate via Azure IMDS endpoint

redesign_required.redesign_rationale: Azure backend pool associations are different from AWS target group attachments.
redesign_required.impact: Medium - requires network interface-level configuration changes

modules_to_create.notes: Consolidate routing and subnet configuration into Azure-native modules
Separate NAT Gateway module, includes public IP for NAT Gateway association
Requires separate network interface configuration with IP settings
NSG association can be to subnet or NIC, might need both
No instance profile needed - use managed identity directly
Requires probe for health checks, separate backend pool resource

migration_risks.description: Existing Azure VNet resources may conflict with migrated VPC CIDR
migration_risks.mitigation: Audit existing VNet address spaces before migration planning

migration_risks.description: Azure public IP SKU determines allocation method (static vs dynamic)
migration_risks.mitigation: Use Standard SKU for static public IP addresses

migration_risks.description: AWS evaluates SG rules implicitly, Azure NSG rules have explicit priority
migration_risks.mitigation: Define explicit priority ordering for NSG rules matching AWS rules
